words.definition: a quick look at something
words.example: I caught a glimpse of a deer running into the woods.

words.definition: from a time long ago, before people wrote things down
words.example: Dinosaurs lived in prehistoric times.

words.definition: from far away
words.example: Scientists can control the robot remotely from a computer.

words.definition: the very top of a mountain
words.example: The climbers cheered when they reached the summit.

words.definition: to grow well and be healthy
words.example: Plants thrive when they get enough sunlight and water.

words.definition: very important; needed
words.example: Water is vital for all living things.

words.definition: able to go under water
words.example: The submersible vehicle dove deep into the ocean.

words.definition: a long, narrow, deep ditch in the ground or ocean floor
words.example: The Mariana Trench is the deepest trench in the ocean.

words.definition: able to work or move by itself without help
words.example: An autonomous robot can clean the floor on its own.

words.definition: when two things crash into each other
words.example: The collision between the two cars made a loud bang.

words.definition: the center or middle of something
words.example: The core of the Earth is very hot.

words.definition: many different kinds
words.example: The rainforest has a diverse group of animals.

words.definition: lasting forever
words.example: The friends made an eternal promise to stay close.

words.definition: not doing anything; not active
words.example: The machine sat idle when nobody was using it.

words.definition: very interesting; making you want to know more
words.example: The mystery book had an intriguing first chapter.

words.definition: coming from living things; grown without chemicals
words.example: We bought organic apples at the farmer's market.

words.definition: to understand something fully
words.example: I can't fathom how the magician did that trick.

words.definition: very strong anger
words.example: The villagers feared the wrath of the storm.

words.definition: a deep crack or opening in the earth
words.example: We looked down into the wide chasm in the rocks.

words.definition: to slowly wear away
words.example: Waves can erode the rocks along the beach.

words.definition: to shine softly with reflected light
words.example: Dewdrops glisten on the grass in the morning.

words.definition: to break suddenly into many pieces
words.example: The glass fell and shattered on the floor.

words.definition: stuck firmly inside something
words.example: The fossil was embedded in the rock.

words.definition: a person who stands guard and keeps watch
words.example: A sentry stood at the gate of the castle.
